Zhangs Notice Of Demurrer And Demurrer To Third Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Feb-07-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Chijeh Hu, Esq. of CJH & Associates PC, T:510.817.0808, for defendants/moving party;
Claudia M. Mourad, Esq. of Hershorin & Henry LLP, T:949.859.5600, for plaintiff/opposing party by Court Call.

RULING: The matter was argued. At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopts the tentative ruling on DEFENDANTS CINDY ZHANG AND RAYMOND ZHANG'S Demurrer To Third Amended Complaint as follows:

Defendants Raymond and Cindy Zhang's demurrer to all four causes of action alleged in the third amended complaint filed by plaintiff Hershorin & Henry, LLP is overruled in its entirety. The TAC alleges sufficient facts for all four claims. The TAC adequately alleges that there was consideration for the indemnity contract because H&H agreed to pay the LLC $44,477.07 notwithstanding its receipt of notice of the attorney's lien. Even though Chicago Title did not sign the agreement, it accepted the agreement by performing because it paid the funds to the LLC through H&H. Nor did the court order mandate the payment to the LLC and, even if it did, there were changed circumstances once the attorney's lien was filed. H&H may plead both breach of contract and fraud arising out of the indemnity agreement. The TAC adequately alleges reasonable reliance based on the payment to the LLC. The TAC also alleges sufficient facts showing that the Zhangs are the alter egos for the LLC.

Prevailing party to prepare a proposed order that repeats the Court's ruling, verbatim, and submit it in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: G. Gonzales; Reporter: Maria Torreano CSR#8600, email: torreanom@aol.com; mtorreano@sftc.org.

MOTION FOR LEAVE TO File A Third Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Oct-30-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF HERSHORIN & HENRY, LLP's, MOTION FOR LEAVE TO File A Third Amended Complaint is GRANTED. While plaintiff may not have been as diligent as it should have been in seeking to amend, the motion is granted because of California's liberal policy of allowing amendments to pleadings and the lack of any legal prejudice to defendants. Defendants' argument about the deficiencies in plaintiff's new claim and allegations are better addressed on a pleading motion such as a demurrer or motion to strike than on a motion for leave to amend.

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)
